note: The ONE tech-comfort vocabulary for a persona's capability profile (capabilities.tech_comfort). An ordered 1-5 scale mirroring friction_levels.json/stance_scale.json; resolved via artifacts.resolve_tech_comfort(). Each level carries a behavioral `hint` — how this persona actually moves through an interface — which session briefs weave into the anti-steering context. `aliases` map free tokens (incl. EN/DE display words) onto a canonical level — add an alias, no code change; lookup is case/whitespace-insensitive. Like friction (and unlike stances) an UNKNOWN level is rejected on write instead of bucketed: silently calling a novice 'comfortable' would corrupt every session simulated from the profile.

items.hint: reads every label before clicking; backs out of anything unfamiliar and gives up rather than guess
sticks to known paths; hesitates at new patterns and re-reads before risking a click
navigates common patterns confidently; slows down and reads only when something surprises
skims labels, tries things, recovers from errors without help
skims, clicks the most prominent CTA, uses shortcuts and expects undo
